channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Samsung is rumored to have changed its Galaxy S26 plans again – and we may not see the flagship phones before March
item.description: Samsung may stick to its traditional naming scheme for these handsets after all, but they may be delayed.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: Tesla's self-driving cars under fire again
item.description: Tesla's Full Self-Driving system faces federal investigation following 58 reports of crashes, with six vehicles running red lights before colliding with other cars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Salesforce chief says he no longer backs Trump sending National Guard to San Francisco
item.description: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff apologized Friday for comments suggesting that President Trump should deploy the National Guard to San Francisco, saying he no longer believes it is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: ChatGPT to allow erotica
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter brings you the latest developments on artificial intelligence, with news on OpenAI moving to soon allow erotica for adult users.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to watch the Orionid meteor shower, debris of Halley's comet
item.description: The Orionid meteor shower peaks this year with the arrival of a new moon, offering a rare chance to see shooting stars without moonlight interference.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: We Rate Dogs slams border agents for allegedly shooting dog
item.description: Popular account We Rate Dogs called border agents "losers" after a family said agents shot their dog in a raid.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, The Hill

item.title: 2 weird but wonderful Android launchers
item.description: I think at this point, it’s safe to say I’m an Android launcher aficionado. (I think it’s also safe to both pat myself on the back and bop myself uponst the nose for coming up with such a delightfully dorky description. But I’ll save that for later.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Pro could be a big disappointment for camera fans
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Pro could have cameras that date back to the S22, but at least its battery might get a boost.
item.dc:creator: James Rogerson, TechRadar

item.title: Uber wants to become the ‘Uber’ of data labeling
item.description: Following its entrance into the data labeling space last year with the launch of a new division, Scaled Solutions, Uber on Thursday introduced a pilot project in the US market that will see its drivers, like their counterparts in India, annotate data for use in AI training.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The internet is now mostly written by machines, study finds
item.description: More new articles online are now written by AI than humans, but most don’t make it into search results.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: Death of kidnapped South Korean student spurs talks with Cambodia to tackle online scams
item.description: PHNOM PENH, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian and South Korean officials agreed Thursday to cooperate in combating online scams, after the death of a South Korean student who was reportedly forced to work in a scam center in Cambodia triggered public outrage in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta to invests $1.5 billion for El Paso AI data center
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) - Facebook's parent company, Meta, is going to be building a new artificial intelligence center in El Paso.
item.dc:creator: Jamel Valencia, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats press Google, YouTube on Trump settlement
item.description: Several Senate Democrats are pressing Google and YouTube for answers about their decision to settle a lawsuit with President Trump over YouTube’s suspension of his account in the wake of the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol. In a Wednesday letter to Google and YouTube executives, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Walmart, OpenAI team up for ChatGPT shopping tool
item.description: Walmart is partnering with OpenAI to allow customers to shop through ChatGPT, the retail giant announced Tuesday. “For many years now, eCommerce shopping experiences have consisted of a search bar and a long list of item responses.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to London in 2026
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Robotaxi pioneer Waymo plans to expand to London next year, marking the company’s latest step in rolling out its driverless ride service internationally.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Major network vendors team to advance Ethernet for scale-up AI networking
item.description: As AI networking technology blossoms, yet another group has formed to make sure Ethernet can handle the stress.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI partners with Broadcom to design its own AI chips
item.description: OpenAI said Monday it is working with chipmaker Broadcom to design its own artificial intelligence computer chips. The two California companies didn't disclose the financial terms of the deal but said they will start deploying the new racks of customized “AI accelerators” late next year.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Crypto spent millions to defeat Sherrod Brown and elect allies. It's ready for a repeat in 2026
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — At a five-star resort tucked in the mountains of Jackson Hole, Wyoming, the cryptocurrency industry was celebrating a historic start to the year on Capitol Hill. Its priorities were sailing through Congress with unusual speed and one senator did not hesitate to say why. Sen.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KREX 5
